message:
feat(core): Add CRISPR pressure analysis and anti-CRISPR candidate detection

Implement CRISPR pressure mapping to identify regions under host CRISPR
immune selection and heuristically predict anti-CRISPR (Acr) protein
candidates based on compositional features.

New module: packages/core/src/crispr.ts (195 lines)

Type Definitions:
- SpacerHit: { position, sequence, host, crisprType, matchScore, pamStatus, strand }
  Represents a detected CRISPR spacer match against a known spacer database

- AcrCandidate: { geneId, geneName, score, family, confidence }
  Represents a predicted anti-CRISPR protein candidate with scoring

- CRISPRPressureWindow: { start, end, pressureIndex, spacerCount, dominantType }
  Represents a genomic window with computed CRISPR pressure score

- CRISPRAnalysisResult: Complete analysis output containing all hits and predictions

Core Algorithm - analyzeCRISPRPressure(sequence, genes):
1. Spacer Hit Detection:
   - Scans sequence for known spacer patterns (mock DB for demo)
   - Validates PAM sequences for Cas9 (downstream NGG) and Cas12a (upstream TTTV)
   - Assigns CRISPR system type (I, II, III, V, VI) based on PAM context
   - Records strand orientation (coding/template)
   - Adds noise hits for visual density on long sequences

2. Anti-CRISPR Candidate Prediction (predictAcrCandidates):
   - Filters genes for typical Acr protein size range (50-200 aa)
   - Translates gene sequences to protein
   - Calculates net charge (basic - acidic residues)
   - Scores based on DNA mimicry heuristics:
     - Net negative charge < -5: +40 points (DNA mimic family)
     - Small size < 100 aa: +20 points
   - Assigns confidence levels based on score thresholds
   - Sorts candidates by score descending

3. Pressure Window Computation (calculatePressure):
   - Divides genome into 500bp windows
   - Sums weighted hit scores within each window:
     - Base: matchScore (0-1)
     - Valid PAM: ×1.5 multiplier
     - Coding strand: ×1.2 multiplier
   - Normalizes all windows to 0-10 scale based on max pressure

PAM Validation Logic:
- Cas9 (Type II): Checks downstream sequence ends with 'GG'
- Cas12a (Type V): Checks upstream sequence starts with 'TTT'
- Other types assigned 'invalid' PAM status

Note: Uses mock spacer database (MOCK_SPACERS) for demonstration.
Production implementation would query a real CRISPR spacer database.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-92s","title":"Foundation: Progressive Disclosure & User Journey","description":"## Progressive Disclosure & User Journey

Strategy for exposing features based on user expertise level.

### New User Journey (First 5 minutes)
- Only Layer 0 (12 sacred controls)
- Help overlay shows essential controls only
- Navigate phages, scroll sequences, toggle DNA/AA

### Intermediate User (First hour)
- Layer 0 + Layer 1 overlays
- Discover overlays via help
- Try diff mode, explore 3D model
- Help now shows 'Advanced: press A for more...'

### Power User
- Full Layer 0-4 access
- Uses command palette (: or Ctrl+P)
- Runs simulations, does comparative analysis
- Discovers features through exploration

### Key Insight
Every feature reachable in ≤3 keypresses but invisible until needed.

Depends on: Help System (phage_explorer-ofm), UI Design System (phage_explorer-dx3)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:35:35.931114-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T00:08:51.333537-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ofm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:01.879645-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-92s","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-dx3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:02.988977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
